function.arguments: {"nextThoughtNeeded":true,"thought":"Architecture decisions: Backend uses Node.js built-in `os` module to get CPU and memory stats. Use Express to serve static files and a REST endpoint. Use Socket.io or Server-Sent Events (SSE) to push real-time updates to the browser. Frontend: single HTML page with Chart.js for live updating charts, styled with modern CSS (dark theme, glassmorphism) to look 'nifty'. Project structure: package.json, server.js, public/index.html, public/css/style.css, public/js/app.js. Phases: 1) Setup & scaffolding (package.json, dependencies), 2) Backend (Express server, os data collection, SSE endpoint), 3) Frontend (HTML structure, CSS styling, JS chart logic), 4) Integration & testing (run server, verify in browser).","thoughtNumber":2,"totalThoughts":3}
